title: Education Discrimination Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Education Discrimination Assistance 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about state and federal laws prohibiting discrimination or harassment in schools and the process of filing a complaint. Staff also answer questions about equal opportunity and nondiscrimination, and provide training, technical assistance and alternate dispute resolution opportunities for students, school districts, parents and interested parties. It is ideal to first alert the local Oregon school district of any concerns and follow its procedures for investigation and resolution. Oregon school districts must adopt written procedures for the prompt resolution of complaints of discrimination or harassment. These procedures are available at any district school or office. District procedures are often printed in school student and parent handbooks. Many districts also post this information on their district or school web sites. 255 Capitol Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces laws to ensure everyone has equal access at work, protects against discrimination, and shields workers who report safety problems or use leave. They also protect people who are looking for housing or using public places like stores and transportation. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 245-3844 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces federal laws that protect people from discrimination based on race, color, national origin, disability status, sex, religion, familial status, or loss of other constitutional rights. Also enforces federal laws that protect individuals from discrimination by suing or prosecuting organizations or individuals who violate civil rights laws. These laws protect individuals from discrimination, harassment, and abuse in settings like housing, the workplace, school, voting, business, healthcare, and public spaces. 950 Pennsylvania Avenue NW Washington, DC 20530 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing discrimination on the basis of race, color, or ethnicity, disability, religion, sex, gender identity, and sexual orientation, immigration or citizenship status, language and/or national origin, family, marital or parental status, age, genetic identification, and service member status Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-2:30pm Email (855) 856-1247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION
